# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }
truefix-session = { path = "crates/truefix-session" }
truefix-transport = { path = "crates/truefix-transport" }
truefix-config = { path = "crates/truefix-config" }
truefix-store = { path = "crates/truefix-store" }
truefix-log = { path = "crates/truefix-log" }

[profile.release]
# Production builds: keep debug info off, optimize. Benchmarks live in benches/.
opt-level = 3

# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J — first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: 🚧 Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** — stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** — the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** — protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields — they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI ─▶ S1 Core codec ─▶ S2 Minimal session ─▶ S3 Recovery/resend ─▶ S4 Data dictionary
   ─▶ S5 Store/log ─▶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect ─▶ S7 All versions + codegen
   ─▶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring ─▶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator ↔ acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) → protocol correctness → production-readiness → feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

# Migration Notes

Breaking-change and upgrade notes for TrueFix. Per Constitution Principle I, breaking changes to the
public API are recorded here with a semantic-version bump (FR-028).

## Unreleased — feature 002 (QuickFIX/J parity completion)

### Application callback signatures are now typed (breaking) — FR-016 / US5

The `Application` trait's fault-returning callbacks change from opaque `Result<(), String>` to typed
outcomes carrying a reason code and optional reference tag:

| Callback | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| `from_admin` | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), Reject>` (logon/admin refusal → 35=3) |
| `to_app`     | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), DoNotSend>` (suppress outbound) |
| `from_app`   | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), BusinessReject>` (→ 35=j) |

`Ok(())` is unchanged. An `Err("...")` becomes the matching typed variant:

- `Reject { reason, ref_tag, text }` — `reason` is the numeric SessionRejectReason (tag 373).
- `DoNotSend` — marker; the engine omits the message and does not store it as sent.
- `BusinessReject { reason, ref_tag, text }` — `reason` is the numeric BusinessRejectReason (tag 380).

These types are exported from `truefix_core` (re-exported by the `truefix` facade). Implementors update
their callback bodies to return the typed variants. No back-compat string variants are retained.

> Status: types landed (Setup/Foundational, stage G-foundational); trait signatures and engine effects
> are wired in stage G5 (US5). This section is finalised in T080 alongside the version bump.

## TrueFix Codebase — Exhaustive Analysis (July 2026)

### Overview
Layered async-tokio FIX engine workspace (edition 2021, MSRV 1.96, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide; `unwrap/expect/panic/indexing_slicing` denied in non-test builds per crate). 9 crates, 90 `.rs` files. All crates at `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`. README still self-labels "pre-implementation (design complete)" but code is substantially implemented.

### a) truefix-core (`crates/truefix-core/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, error, message, field, field_map, group, tags, cracker, factory, framing, codec/{mod,decode,encode}; tests: field_types, garbled, groups, reference_vectors, roundtrip, versions; bench: codec.rs.
- **Types**: `Field` (raw bytes, typed accessors: as_str/int/decimal/char/bool/utc_timestamp; picosecond-accepting timestamp parser truncating to ns), `FieldMap` (ordered `Member::{Field, Group}` preserving wire order), `Group`+`GroupSpec` trait, `Message` (header/body/trailer FieldMaps).
- **Errors** (`error.rs`): `FieldError`, `DecodeError`, **`Reject`** (struct: reason/ref_tag/text — SessionRejectReason), **`DoNotSend`** (struct, suppresses send+seq), **`BusinessReject`** (struct: reason/ref_tag/text — BusinessRejectReason). All three exist as **typed** error structs (FR-016). `FieldNotFound`/`RejectLogon` type names do NOT exist (RejectLogon absent; field-not-found is handled via dict `RejectReason::RequiredTagMissing`, not a type).
- **MessageCracker** (`cracker.rs`): **skeleton only** — a trait with `fn crack(&mut self, message: &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>`; docstring says "Full typed dispatch over the generated messages arrives with the dictionary codegen (Stage S4)". NOT typed.
- **MessageFactory** (`factory.rs`): skeleton trait only.
- **Codec**: `decode` (flat fields), `decode_with_groups` (dict-driven group parsing via `GroupSpec` — works, greedy, recursive nested groups), `encode` (computes BodyLength/CheckSum, canonical order). `decode_with_dict` name does NOT exist (the equivalent is `decode_with_groups`). Group parsing from wire works.
- **Binary data fields**: `data_field_for_length` handles length→data field pairs (RawDataLength/RawData etc.); tokenizer honors embedded SOH.
- **framing.rs**: `frame_length` via BodyLength + 7-byte CheckSum trailer.
- **No reverse-route helper** in core.

### b) truefix-dict (`crates/truefix-dict/src/*.rs`, build.rs)
Files: lib, model, parser, validate, fixt, hash, build.rs; dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict (FIX40/41/42/43/44/FIXT11/FIX50/FIX50SP1/FIX50SP2). Tests: dual_track, fixt, group_decode, group_validation, parse, rejection_layers, toggles, versions.
- **Codegen** (`build.rs`): generates per-version `*_DICT_HASH: u64` (FNV-1a) + `pub mod {version}_msgs { pub const NEW_ORDER_SINGLE: &str = "D"; ... }` — **MsgType string constants only, NO typed structs**. Runtime asserts parsed hash == generated hash (dual-track proof, `dual_track` test).
- **Model** (`model.rs`): `DataDictionary` (fields/messages/header/trailer/groups/hash), `FieldDef`, `FieldType` (17 variants), `MessageDef` (required/optional/member_tags), `GroupDef` (count_tag/delimiter/members), `RejectReason` enum (10 variants with `.code()`), `ValidationError` (session vs business flag), **`ValidationOptions`** struct.
- **Validation toggles present in ValidationOptions**: `validate_fields_have_values`, `validate_user_defined_fields`, `allow_unknown_msg_fields`, `check_required_fields`, `check_field_types`, `check_groups`, **`first_field_in_group_is_delimiter`** ✓, **`validate_unordered_group_fields`** ✓. NOTE: `CheckCompID` and `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder` are NOT in ValidationOptions (out-of-order top-level fields not validated here).
- **validate.rs**: `impl GroupSpec for DataDictionary`; `validate()` does two rejection layers (session-level for tag/format/enum/required/group-structure; business-level for unknown MsgType). Recursive nested-group validation with delimiter + ordering checks.
- **FIXT** (`fixt.rs`): `FixtDictionaries` (transport + BTreeMap of application dicts by ApplVerID + DefaultApplVerID fallback + `application_for()`).
- **Custom dictionary loading**: `parse(&str)` from any normalized source; bundled `load_fixNN()` helpers; `ALL_DICTS` const. (Custom XML/.dict loading is NOT present — only the normalized `.fixdict` format.)
- **Component model**: NO `Component` type — groups only (no FIX component reusability abstraction).
- **FIX Latest / fixlatest**: NOT present (versions are FIX40–FIX50SP2 + FIXT11).

### c) truefix-session (`crates/truefix-session/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, config, state, admin, application, schedule, session_id, tags, time_util; tests: state_machine, recovery, restart_resend, validation_hook, timeouts, schedule.
- **SessionConfig** (`config.rs`) contains: begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, role, heartbeat_interval, reset_on_logon/logout/disconnect, refresh_on_logon, **persist_messages** ✓, resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency, max_latency, **check_comp_id** ✓ (field exists, default true), heartbeat_timeout_multiplier ✓, **timestamp_precision** (enum TimeStampPrecision: Seconds/Milli/Micro/Nano) ✓, logon_timeout, logout_timeout, reconnect_interval, schedule.
- **MessageStore integration**: NOT in Session — Session uses an in-memory `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` store + `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` out-of-order queue. The persistent `MessageStore` is wired in at the **transport** layer (`Services.store`), which calls `session.seed_sequences()` and `session.seed_sent_messages()` on connect, and persists via `persist_sent()`. So persistence is transport-driven, not Session-owned.
- **state.rs (sans-IO state machine)**: `Event::{Connected, Received, Tick, StartLogout}`, `Action::{Send, Disconnect}`, `SessionState::{Disconnected, AwaitingLogon, LoggedOn, AwaitingLogout}`. Implements: logon (initiator/acceptor, ResetSeqNumFlag, HeartBtInt adoption, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum 789 resend), seq-gap detection (too-high queues + single ResendRequest with resend-pending guard; too-low → Logout+Disconnect unless PossDup), ResendRequest handling (admin→GapFill, app→PossDup replay with OrigSendingTime), SequenceReset-GapFill/Reset, out-of-order queue drain, TestRequest→Heartbeat, idle Heartbeat timer, heartbeat-timeout disconnect (2*hb+2 ticks), CheckLatency (stale SendingTime→Logout), dictionary validation hook (`validate_app` → session Reject or BusinessMessageReject).
- **RejectGarbledMessage**: NOT implemented — transport `drain_messages` silently drops garbled frames ("Garbled frames are dropped at this stage; RejectGarbledMessage arrives in S3" — still a TODO). `CheckCompID`: config field exists but I found no comp-id validation enforcement in state.rs (latency checked; comp-id appears unenforced). `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`: not enforced at session level.
- **Switches honored**: PersistMessages ✓, HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier ✓ (hardcoded `2*hb+2` rather than using config field — config field set but logic uses literal `2`), TimeStampPrecision ✓ (time_util.rs formats per precision, though `now_utc_timestamp()` defaults to Milliseconds and `now_utc_timestamp_prec` exists), NOT honored: SendRedundantResendRequests (no field), ClosedResendInterval (no field), ResetOnError (no field), DisconnectOnError (no field), DisableHeartBeatCheck (no field), TestRequestDelayMultiplier (no field). These are all listed as `Rec` (Recognized) in config keys.rs.
- **schedule.rs**: `Schedule` (non_stop / daily start-end window wrapping midnight / weekdays filter / utc_offset_seconds). `is_in_session()`. No named IANA timezones (whole-second offset only); no StartDay/EndDay weekly windows.
- **schedule_reset.rs**: does NOT exist as a file. Scheduled reset handled inline in transport's `run_scheduled_initiator` (reset store on entering a window).
- **Reverse routing**: NOT implemented (no reverse_route helper; comp IDs are stamped from config).
- **admin.rs**: builders for Logon/Logout/Heartbeat/TestRequest/ResendRequest/SequenceReset/Reject/reject_with_reason/business_message_reject/prepare_resend.

### d) truefix-store (`crates/truefix-store/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, file, memory, noop, sql; test: restart_resend.
- **Backends**: `MemoryStore`, `FileStore`, `CachedFileStore`, `NoopStore`, `SqlStore` (behind `sql` feature). `MessageStore` trait (next_sender/target_seq, set_*, save, get, reset). `StoreConfig` enum + `build_store()`.
- **FileStoreSync**: NOT honored — `FileStore` uses synchronous std `fs` append (`OpenOptions::append`) on every `save`; `FileStoreSync` is `Rec` in keys.rs, not implemented; no fsync control.
- **CachedFileStore**: differentiated as a wrapper struct `CachedFileStore(FileStore)` but **delegates entirely** to FileStore (identical impl, no write-batching yet — docstring says optimizations "will live" there).
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore**: partially — `FileStore::was_corrupted()` flag + corrupt-trailing-record recovery on open (`parse_records` tolerates truncation). `ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore` key itself is `Rec`, not wired as a switch.
- **SQL**: `SqlStore` is **SQLite-only** (`SqliteConnectOptions`/`SqlitePool`), despite Cargo.toml enabling `postgres`+`mysql` sqlx features. NO PG/MySQL code paths. Hardcoded table names `seqnums`/`messages` (no custom table names — `JdbcStoreMessagesTableName` etc. are `Rec`, unused). `SqlitePool::connect_with` only — **no explicit connection-pool sizing config** (JdbcMaxActiveConnection etc. are `Rec`, unused).

### e) truefix-log (`crates/truefix-log/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, file, screen, composite, tracing_log, sql; test: composite, sql_log.
- `Log` trait (on_incoming/on_outgoing/on_event, sync+infallible). `LogConfig` enum + `build_log()`. `ScreenLog`/`FileLog`(messages.log+event.log)/`TracingLog`/`CompositeLog`/`SqlLog`(sql feature).
- **Log config switches NOT implemented**: `FileLogHeartbeats`, `ScreenLogShowEvents`, `ScreenLogShowHeartBeats`, `ScreenLogShowIncoming`, `ScreenLogShowOutgoing`, `FileIncludeMilliseconds`, etc. are all `Rec` in keys.rs and NOT honored — ScreenLog/FileLog unconditionally log all streams. No filtering.
- **SQL log**: SQLite-only (`SqlLog` writes to `log_messages`/`log_events` via a background tokio task over an unbounded mpsc channel). NO PG/MySQL. No custom table names (`JdbcLogIncomingTable` etc. `Rec`, unused).

### f) truefix-transport (`crates/truefix-transport/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, framing; tests: auth, integration_logon, monitor, multi_dynamic, reconnect, restart_continuity, scheduled, tls.
- **Initiator/Acceptor**: `connect_initiator`, `connect_initiator_with`, `connect_initiator_tls` (rustls ClientConfig + SNI ServerName), `connect_initiator_reconnecting` (ReconnectHandle), `run_scheduled_initiator` (ScheduledHandle), `Acceptor` (single static session), `AcceptorBuilder` (multi-session registry + dynamic template + allow-listing + optional TLS ServerConfig via `with_tls`).
- **Failover/multi-endpoint**: NOT present — no `SocketConnectHost1`, no `alternate`, no `ConnectEndpointSet`/`connect_endpoints`. Single connect address only.
- **Proxy protocol**: NOT present — `ProxyType`/`ProxyHost` etc. are `Unsupported` in keys.rs ("HTTP/SOCKS proxy is not yet implemented").
- **mTLS**: `AcceptorBuilder::with_tls(Arc<rustls::ServerConfig>)` accepts a pre-built ServerConfig (tests use `rcgen` to mint certs), and `NeedClientAuth` key is `Impl`, but **there is no parsing of TLS config from settings files** — `rustls-pemfile` is in workspace deps but **NOT used anywhere in crates/** (no pemfile code; configs are built by tests/callers, not from `.cfg`). TLS is "implemented" only as pass-through rustls configs.
- **Socket options**: `SocketOptions { tcp_no_delay }` only. Full socket options (KeepAlive, ReuseAddress, Linger, ReceiveBufferSize, SendBufferSize, etc.) are `Rec`/unimplemented.
- **Metrics exporter**: NOT present — `metrics` is in workspace deps but **NOT depended on by any crate** (no `metrics = workspace` in any crate Cargo.toml; no `metrics::` usage). No metrics exporter.
- **Services**: store/log/socket_options/monitor/validator. `Monitor` (status/is_connected/sessions/force_logout/reset/send_app). Persistent store wired for restart continuity (seed sequences + rehydrate sent messages).
- **framing.rs**: same `frame_length` as core.

### g) truefix-config (`crates/truefix-config/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, builder, keys; tests: config_errors, key_coverage.
- `SessionSettings::parse` ([DEFAULT]/[SESSION] dialect, `${name}` interpolation, comments). `ConfigError` enum (Malformed/UnresolvedVariable/MissingRequired/InvalidValue/UnknownConnectionType) — **typed** ✓.
- **builder.rs**: `SessionSettings::resolve() -> Vec<ResolvedSession>` (all-or-nothing). Maps keys → `SessionConfig` (HeartBtInt, ResetOnLogon/Logout/Disconnect, PersistMessages, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, LogonTimeout, LogoutTimeout, ReconnectInterval, ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed, EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum, NonStopSession) + `ConnectionType` + address (SocketAcceptAddress/Port, SocketConnectHost/Port) + `StoreConfig` (FileStorePath→File, else Memory). `EngineConfig` name does NOT exist (facade uses `Engine` instead).
- **keys.rs** (`APPENDIX_A_KEYS`): comprehensive Appendix A registry with `Stance::{Implemented, Recognized, Unsupported}`. Implemented: BeginString, SenderCompID, TargetCompID, ConnectionType, DefaultApplVerID, UseDataDictionary, DataDictionary, AppDataDictionary, TransportDataDictionary, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, ValidateUnorderedGroupFields, ValidateUserDefinedFields, ValidateIncomingMessage, ValidateSequenceNumbers, ValidateChecksum, AllowUnknownMsgFields, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, RejectInvalidMessage, FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter, HeartBtInt, Logon/LogoutTimeout, ResetOnLogon/Logout/Disconnect, PersistMessages, ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLast/Next..., RequiresOrigSendingTime, AllowPosDup, StartTime, EndTime, Weekdays, TimeZone, NonStopSession, SocketAcceptAddress/Port, AcceptorTemplate, DynamicSession, AllowedRemoteAddresses, SocketConnectHost/Port, ReconnectInterval, SocketTcpNoDelay, SocketUseSSL, EnabledProtocols, CipherSuites, SocketKeyStore(+Password), SocketTrustStore(+Password), NeedClientAuth, UseSNI, SNIHostName, FileStorePath, FileLogPath. Recognized (parsed/partial): SenderSubID/LocationID, SessionQualifier, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, CheckCompID, RejectGarbledMessage, HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier, DisableHeartBeatCheck, TestRequestDelayMultiplier, ResetOnError, RefreshOnLogon, SendRedundantResendRequests, ClosedResendInterval, ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore, DisconnectOnError, TimeStampPrecision, StartDay/EndDay, Jdbc* (all SQL keys), FileStoreSync, FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs, all FileLog/ScreenLog switches. Unsupported: Proxy* ("proxy not implemented"), SLF4J* ("tracing facade"), Jndi* ("JNDI not applicable"), Sleepycat* ("deferred for v1").

### h) truefix-at (`crates/truefix-at/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, runner, scenarios; test: conformance. `SUITE_VERSIONS = ["FIX.4.2", "FIX.4.4"]`.
- **EXACT scenario count**: The `server_suite()` function pushes scenarios. Counting: for each of 2 versions → 23 scenarios (valid_logon, logon_seq_too_high, msgseqnum_too_high, msgseqnum_too_low, received_test_request, unsolicited_logout, logon_response_carries_reset_flag, logon_adopts_heartbeat_interval, resend_request_gap_fill, resend_request_bounded_end, resend_request_not_duplicated, resend_request_begin_zero_ignored, out_of_order_queued_then_drained, sequence_reset_reset, sequence_reset_gap_fill_advances, sequence_reset_gap_fill_backward_ignored, resend_request_nothing_to_resend, reject_message_consumed, heartbeat_consumed, missing_msg_seq_num, idle_heartbeat_emitted, test_request_on_silence, poss_dup_too_low) = 46. Then FIX.4.2-only field-validation: 7 (valid_new_order_accepted_42, required_field_missing_42, incorrect_enum_value_42, invalid_tag_number_42, tag_not_defined_for_msg_type_42, tag_specified_without_value_42, incorrect_data_format_42). Then FIX.4.4-only: 18 (valid_new_order_accepted, group_count_mismatch, group_out_of_order, nested_group_missing_delimiter, group_zero_count, app_order_executed, app_orders_sequenced, app_message_resent_as_poss_dup, app_mixed_resend_gapfill_then_possdup, acceptor_initiated_logout, required_field_missing, incorrect_enum_value, invalid_tag_number, tag_not_defined_for_msg_type, tag_specified_without_value, incorrect_data_format, unregistered_msg_type) — note that's 17 listed; let me recount the 4.4 block: valid_new_order_accepted, group_count_mismatch, group_out_of_order, nested_group_missing_delimiter, group_zero_count, app_order_executed, app_orders_sequenced, app_message_resent_as_poss_dup, app_mixed_resend_gapfill_then_possdup, acceptor_initiated_logout, required_field_missing, incorrect_enum_value, invalid_tag_number, tag_not_defined_for_msg_type, tag_specified_without_value, incorrect_data_format, unregistered_msg_type = 17. Then 5 special (next_expected_msg_seq_num, last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency_timestamps, garbled_message_dropped, resend_request_chunk_size). **TOTAL = 46 + 7 + 17 + 5 = 75 scenarios.** (Each scenario runs against its `versions` vec — most version-parameterized ones carry a single version, so 75 scenario definitions → 75 (scenario,version) runs since the per-version ones are pre-split.)
- **Full scenario name list** (every name): 1a_ValidLogonWithCorrectMsgSeqNum, 1a_ValidLogonMsgSeqNumTooHigh, 2b_MsgSeqNumTooHigh, 2c_MsgSeqNumTooLow, 4b_ReceivedTestRequest, 13b_UnsolicitedLogoutMessage, 1d_LogonResponseResetFlag, 1c_LogonAdoptsHeartBtInt, 2f_ResendRequestGapFill, 2f3_ResendRequestBoundedEnd, 2_ResendRequestNotDuplicated, 2_ResendRequestBeginZeroIgnored, 2x_OutOfOrderQueuedThenDrained, 2g_SequenceResetReset, 2d_SequenceResetGapFillAdvances, 2e_SequenceResetGapFillBackwardIgnored, 2h_ResendRequestNothingToResend, 3a_ReceivedRejectConsumed, 0a_ReceivedHeartbeatConsumed, 2_MissingMsgSeqNum, 0_IdleHeartbeatEmitted, 4_TestRequestOnSilence, 2m_PossDupMsgSeqNumTooLow (×2 versions); 14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted_42, 14b_RequiredFieldMissing_42, 14e_IncorrectEnumValue_42, 14a_InvalidTagNumber_42, 14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType_42, 14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue_42, 14f_IncorrectDataFormat_42; 14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted, 14i_RepeatingGroupCountNotEqual, 14j_OutOfOrderRepeatingGroupMembers, QFJ934_MissingDelimiterNestedRepeatingGroup, 21_RepeatingGroupSpecifierWithValueOfZero, app_NewOrderSingleExecuted, app_OrdersOutboundSequenced, app_MessageResentAsPossDup, app_MixedResendGapFillThenPossDup, 5_AcceptorInitiatedLogout, 14b_RequiredFieldMissing, 14e_IncorrectEnumValue, 14a_InvalidTagNumber, 14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType, 14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue, 14f_IncorrectDataFormat, 2r_UnregisteredMsgType; special_NextExpectedMsgSeqNum, special_LastMsgSeqNumProcessed, special_CheckLatencyStaleSendingTime, special_GarbledMessageDropped, special_ResendRequestChunkSize.
- **Versions**: FIX.4.2 and FIX.4.4 only (FIX 4.0/4.1/4.3/4.4/5.0/SP1/SP2/FIXT NOT in AT suite).
- **Categories**: logon/sequencing, admin handling, resend/gap-fill, out-of-order queueing, field-validation reject family (4.2 + 4.4), special suites (789 NextExpectedMsgSeqNum, 369 LastMsgSeqNumProcessed, CheckLatency, garbled/checksum, resendRequestChunkSize), application-execution scenarios (executor app replying ExecutionReports). Runner is black-box over a real transport acceptor (fresh acceptor per scenario/version with per-scenario `SessionTweaks`).

### i) truefix facade (`crates/truefix/src/lib.rs`, examples/)

### j) New crates/modules
No crates beyond the 9 listed. thrdpty/ exists at workspace root (third-party, outside crates/ — not read per scope).

- **proxy/PROXY protocol**: ABSENT (Unsupported).
- **mongo/MongoDB**: ABSENT.

Files: lib, error, message, field, field_map, group, tags, cracker, factory, framing, codec/{mod,decode,encode}; tests: field_types, garbled, groups, reference_vectors, roundtrip, versions; bench: codec.rs.
- **Types**: `Field` (raw bytes, typed accessors: as_str/int/decimal/char/bool/utc_timestamp; picosecond-accepting timestamp parser truncating to ns), `FieldMap` (ordered `Member::{Field, Group}` preserving wire order), `Group`+`GroupSpec` trait, `Message` (header/body/trailer FieldMaps).
- **Errors** (`error.rs`): `FieldError`, `DecodeError`, **`Reject`** (struct: reason/ref_tag/text — SessionRejectReason), **`DoNotSend`** (struct, suppresses send+seq), **`BusinessReject`** (struct: reason/ref_tag/text — BusinessRejectReason). All three exist as **typed** error structs (FR-016). `FieldNotFound`/`RejectLogon` type names do NOT exist (RejectLogon absent; field-not-found is handled via dict `RejectReason::RequiredTagMissing`, not a type).
- **MessageCracker** (`cracker.rs`): **skeleton only** — a trait with `fn crack(&mut self, message: &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>`; docstring says "Full typed dispatch over the generated messages arrives with the dictionary codegen (Stage S4)". NOT typed.
- **MessageFactory** (`factory.rs`): skeleton trait only.
- **Codec**: `decode` (flat fields), `decode_with_groups` (dict-driven group parsing via `GroupSpec` — works, greedy, recursive nested groups), `encode` (computes BodyLength/CheckSum, canonical order). `decode_with_dict` name does NOT exist (the equivalent is `decode_with_groups`). Group parsing from wire works.
- **Binary data fields**: `data_field_for_length` handles length→data field pairs (RawDataLength/RawData etc.); tokenizer honors embedded SOH.
- **framing.rs**: `frame_length` via BodyLength + 7-byte CheckSum trailer.
- **No reverse-route helper** in core.

### b) truefix-dict (`crates/truefix-dict/src/*.rs`, build.rs)
Files: lib, model, parser, validate, fixt, hash, build.rs; dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict (FIX40/41/42/43/44/FIXT11/FIX50/FIX50SP1/FIX50SP2). Tests: dual_track, fixt, group_decode, group_validation, parse, rejection_layers, toggles, versions.
- **Codegen** (`build.rs`): generates per-version `*_DICT_HASH: u64` (FNV-1a) + `pub mod {version}_msgs { pub const NEW_ORDER_SINGLE: &str = "D"; ... }` — **MsgType string constants only, NO typed structs**. Runtime asserts parsed hash == generated hash (dual-track proof, `dual_track` test).
- **Model** (`model.rs`): `DataDictionary` (fields/messages/header/trailer/groups/hash), `FieldDef`, `FieldType` (17 variants), `MessageDef` (required/optional/member_tags), `GroupDef` (count_tag/delimiter/members), `RejectReason` enum (10 variants with `.code()`), `ValidationError` (session vs business flag), **`ValidationOptions`** struct.
- **Validation toggles present in ValidationOptions**: `validate_fields_have_values`, `validate_user_defined_fields`, `allow_unknown_msg_fields`, `check_required_fields`, `check_field_types`, `check_groups`, **`first_field_in_group_is_delimiter`** ✓, **`validate_unordered_group_fields`** ✓. NOTE: `CheckCompID` and `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder` are NOT in ValidationOptions (out-of-order top-level fields not validated here).
- **validate.rs**: `impl GroupSpec for DataDictionary`; `validate()` does two rejection layers (session-level for tag/format/enum/required/group-structure; business-level for unknown MsgType). Recursive nested-group validation with delimiter + ordering checks.
- **FIXT** (`fixt.rs`): `FixtDictionaries` (transport + BTreeMap of application dicts by ApplVerID + DefaultApplVerID fallback + `application_for()`).
- **Custom dictionary loading**: `parse(&str)` from any normalized source; bundled `load_fixNN()` helpers; `ALL_DICTS` const. (Custom XML/.dict loading is NOT present — only the normalized `.fixdict` format.)
- **Component model**: NO `Component` type — groups only (no FIX component reusability abstraction).
- **FIX Latest / fixlatest**: NOT present (versions are FIX40–FIX50SP2 + FIXT11).

### c) truefix-session (`crates/truefix-session/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, config, state, admin, application, schedule, session_id, tags, time_util; tests: state_machine, recovery, restart_resend, validation_hook, timeouts, schedule.
- **SessionConfig** (`config.rs`) contains: begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, role, heartbeat_interval, reset_on_logon/logout/disconnect, refresh_on_logon, **persist_messages** ✓, resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency, max_latency, **check_comp_id** ✓ (field exists, default true), heartbeat_timeout_multiplier ✓, **timestamp_precision** (enum TimeStampPrecision: Seconds/Milli/Micro/Nano) ✓, logon_timeout, logout_timeout, reconnect_interval, schedule.
- **MessageStore integration**: NOT in Session — Session uses an in-memory `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` store + `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` out-of-order queue. The persistent `MessageStore` is wired in at the **transport** layer (`Services.store`), which calls `session.seed_sequences()` and `session.seed_sent_messages()` on connect, and persists via `persist_sent()`. So persistence is transport-driven, not Session-owned.
- **state.rs (sans-IO state machine)**: `Event::{Connected, Received, Tick, StartLogout}`, `Action::{Send, Disconnect}`, `SessionState::{Disconnected, AwaitingLogon, LoggedOn, AwaitingLogout}`. Implements: logon (initiator/acceptor, ResetSeqNumFlag, HeartBtInt adoption, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum 789 resend), seq-gap detection (too-high queues + single ResendRequest with resend-pending guard; too-low → Logout+Disconnect unless PossDup), ResendRequest handling (admin→GapFill, app→PossDup replay with OrigSendingTime), SequenceReset-GapFill/Reset, out-of-order queue drain, TestRequest→Heartbeat, idle Heartbeat timer, heartbeat-timeout disconnect (2*hb+2 ticks), CheckLatency (stale SendingTime→Logout), dictionary validation hook (`validate_app` → session Reject or BusinessMessageReject).
- **RejectGarbledMessage**: NOT implemented — transport `drain_messages` silently drops garbled frames ("Garbled frames are dropped at this stage; RejectGarbledMessage arrives in S3" — still a TODO). `CheckCompID`: config field exists but I found no comp-id validation enforcement in state.rs (latency checked; comp-id appears unenforced). `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`: not enforced at session level.
- **Switches honored**: PersistMessages ✓, HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier ✓ (hardcoded `2*hb+2` rather than using config field — config field set but logic uses literal `2`), TimeStampPrecision ✓ (time_util.rs formats per precision, though `now_utc_timestamp()` defaults to Milliseconds and `now_utc_timestamp_prec` exists), NOT honored: SendRedundantResendRequests (no field), ClosedResendInterval (no field), ResetOnError (no field), DisconnectOnError (no field), DisableHeartBeatCheck (no field), TestRequestDelayMultiplier (no field). These are all listed as `Rec` (Recognized) in config keys.rs.
- **schedule.rs**: `Schedule` (non_stop / daily start-end window wrapping midnight / weekdays filter / utc_offset_seconds). `is_in_session()`. No named IANA timezones (whole-second offset only); no StartDay/EndDay weekly windows.
- **schedule_reset.rs**: does NOT exist as a file. Scheduled reset handled inline in transport's `run_scheduled_initiator` (reset store on entering a window).
- **Reverse routing**: NOT implemented (no reverse_route helper; comp IDs are stamped from config).
- **admin.rs**: builders for Logon/Logout/Heartbeat/TestRequest/ResendRequest/SequenceReset/Reject/reject_with_reason/business_message_reject/prepare_resend.

### d) truefix-store (`crates/truefix-store/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, file, memory, noop, sql; test: restart_resend.
- **Backends**: `MemoryStore`, `FileStore`, `CachedFileStore`, `NoopStore`, `SqlStore` (behind `sql` feature). `MessageStore` trait (next_sender/target_seq, set_*, save, get, reset). `StoreConfig` enum + `build_store()`.
- **FileStoreSync**: NOT honored — `FileStore` uses synchronous std `fs` append (`OpenOptions::append`) on every `save`; `FileStoreSync` is `Rec` in keys.rs, not implemented; no fsync control.
- **CachedFileStore**: differentiated as a wrapper struct `CachedFileStore(FileStore)` but **delegates entirely** to FileStore (identical impl, no write-batching yet — docstring says optimizations "will live" there).
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore**: partially — `FileStore::was_corrupted()` flag + corrupt-trailing-record recovery on open (`parse_records` tolerates truncation). `ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore` key itself is `Rec`, not wired as a switch.
- **SQL**: `SqlStore` is **SQLite-only** (`SqliteConnectOptions`/`SqlitePool`), despite Cargo.toml enabling `postgres`+`mysql` sqlx features. NO PG/MySQL code paths. Hardcoded table names `seqnums`/`messages` (no custom table names — `JdbcStoreMessagesTableName` etc. are `Rec`, unused). `SqlitePool::connect_with` only — **no explicit connection-pool sizing config** (JdbcMaxActiveConnection etc. are `Rec`, unused).

### e) truefix-log (`crates/truefix-log/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, file, screen, composite, tracing_log, sql; test: composite, sql_log.
- `Log` trait (on_incoming/on_outgoing/on_event, sync+infallible). `LogConfig` enum + `build_log()`. `ScreenLog`/`FileLog`(messages.log+event.log)/`TracingLog`/`CompositeLog`/`SqlLog`(sql feature).
- **Log config switches NOT implemented**: `FileLogHeartbeats`, `ScreenLogShowEvents`, `ScreenLogShowHeartBeats`, `ScreenLogShowIncoming`, `ScreenLogShowOutgoing`, `FileIncludeMilliseconds`, etc. are all `Rec` in keys.rs and NOT honored — ScreenLog/FileLog unconditionally log all streams. No filtering.
- **SQL log**: SQLite-only (`SqlLog` writes to `log_messages`/`log_events` via a background tokio task over an unbounded mpsc channel). NO PG/MySQL. No custom table names (`JdbcLogIncomingTable` etc. `Rec`, unused).

### f) truefix-transport (`crates/truefix-transport/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, framing; tests: auth, integration_logon, monitor, multi_dynamic, reconnect, restart_continuity, scheduled, tls.
- **Initiator/Acceptor**: `connect_initiator`, `connect_initiator_with`, `connect_initiator_tls` (rustls ClientConfig + SNI ServerName), `connect_initiator_reconnecting` (ReconnectHandle), `run_scheduled_initiator` (ScheduledHandle), `Acceptor` (single static session), `AcceptorBuilder` (multi-session registry + dynamic template + allow-listing + optional TLS ServerConfig via `with_tls`).
- **Failover/multi-endpoint**: NOT present — no `SocketConnectHost1`, no `alternate`, no `ConnectEndpointSet`/`connect_endpoints`. Single connect address only.
- **Proxy protocol**: NOT present — `ProxyType`/`ProxyHost` etc. are `Unsupported` in keys.rs ("HTTP/SOCKS proxy is not yet implemented").
- **mTLS**: `AcceptorBuilder::with_tls(Arc<rustls::ServerConfig>)` accepts a pre-built ServerConfig (tests use `rcgen` to mint certs), and `NeedClientAuth` key is `Impl`, but **there is no parsing of TLS config from settings files** — `rustls-pemfile` is in workspace deps but **NOT used anywhere in crates/** (no pemfile code; configs are built by tests/callers, not from `.cfg`). TLS is "implemented" only as pass-through rustls configs.
- **Socket options**: `SocketOptions { tcp_no_delay }` only. Full socket options (KeepAlive, ReuseAddress, Linger, ReceiveBufferSize, SendBufferSize, etc.) are `Rec`/unimplemented.
- **Metrics exporter**: NOT present — `metrics` is in workspace deps but **NOT depended on by any crate** (no `metrics = workspace` in any crate Cargo.toml; no `metrics::` usage). No metrics exporter.
- **Services**: store/log/socket_options/monitor/validator. `Monitor` (status/is_connected/sessions/force_logout/reset/send_app). Persistent store wired for restart continuity (seed sequences + rehydrate sent messages).
- **framing.rs**: same `frame_length` as core.

### g) truefix-config (`crates/truefix-config/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, builder, keys; tests: config_errors, key_coverage.
- `SessionSettings::parse` ([DEFAULT]/[SESSION] dialect, `${name}` interpolation, comments). `ConfigError` enum (Malformed/UnresolvedVariable/MissingRequired/InvalidValue/UnknownConnectionType) — **typed** ✓.
- **builder.rs**: `SessionSettings::resolve() -> Vec<ResolvedSession>` (all-or-nothing). Maps keys → `SessionConfig` (HeartBtInt, ResetOnLogon/Logout/Disconnect, PersistMessages, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, LogonTimeout, LogoutTimeout, ReconnectInterval, ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed, EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum, NonStopSession) + `ConnectionType` + address (SocketAcceptAddress/Port, SocketConnectHost/Port) + `StoreConfig` (FileStorePath→File, else Memory). `EngineConfig` name does NOT exist (facade uses `Engine` instead).
- **keys.rs** (`APPENDIX_A_KEYS`): comprehensive Appendix A registry with `Stance::{Implemented, Recognized, Unsupported}`. Implemented: BeginString, SenderCompID, TargetCompID, ConnectionType, DefaultApplVerID, UseDataDictionary, DataDictionary, AppDataDictionary, TransportDataDictionary, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, ValidateUnorderedGroupFields, ValidateUserDefinedFields, ValidateIncomingMessage, ValidateSequenceNumbers, ValidateChecksum, AllowUnknownMsgFields, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, RejectInvalidMessage, FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter, HeartBtInt, Logon/LogoutTimeout, ResetOnLogon/Logout/Disconnect, PersistMessages, ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLast/Next..., RequiresOrigSendingTime, AllowPosDup, StartTime, EndTime, Weekdays, TimeZone, NonStopSession, SocketAcceptAddress/Port, AcceptorTemplate, DynamicSession, AllowedRemoteAddresses, SocketConnectHost/Port, ReconnectInterval, SocketTcpNoDelay, SocketUseSSL, EnabledProtocols, CipherSuites, SocketKeyStore(+Password), SocketTrustStore(+Password), NeedClientAuth, UseSNI, SNIHostName, FileStorePath, FileLogPath. Recognized (parsed/partial): SenderSubID/LocationID, SessionQualifier, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, CheckCompID, RejectGarbledMessage, HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier, DisableHeartBeatCheck, TestRequestDelayMultiplier, ResetOnError, RefreshOnLogon, SendRedundantResendRequests, ClosedResendInterval, ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore, DisconnectOnError, TimeStampPrecision, StartDay/EndDay, Jdbc* (all SQL keys), FileStoreSync, FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs, all FileLog/ScreenLog switches. Unsupported: Proxy* ("proxy not implemented"), SLF4J* ("tracing facade"), Jndi* ("JNDI not applicable"), Sleepycat* ("deferred for v1").

### h) truefix-at (`crates/truefix-at/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, runner, scenarios; test: conformance. `SUITE_VERSIONS = ["FIX.4.2", "FIX.4.4"]`.
- **EXACT scenario count**: The `server_suite()` function pushes scenarios. Counting: for each of 2 versions → 23 scenarios (valid_logon, logon_seq_too_high, msgseqnum_too_high, msgseqnum_too_low, received_test_request, unsolicited_logout, logon_response_carries_reset_flag, logon_adopts_heartbeat_interval, resend_request_gap_fill, resend_request_bounded_end, resend_request_not_duplicated, resend_request_begin_zero_ignored, out_of_order_queued_then_drained, sequence_reset_reset, sequence_reset_gap_fill_advances, sequence_reset_gap_fill_backward_ignored, resend_request_nothing_to_resend, reject_message_consumed, heartbeat_consumed, missing_msg_seq_num, idle_heartbeat_emitted, test_request_on_silence, poss_dup_too_low) = 46. Then FIX.4.2-only field-validation: 7 (valid_new_order_accepted_42, required_field_missing_42, incorrect_enum_value_42, invalid_tag_number_42, tag_not_defined_for_msg_type_42, tag_specified_without_value_42, incorrect_data_format_42). Then FIX.4.4-only: 18 (valid_new_order_accepted, group_count_mismatch, group_out_of_order, nested_group_missing_delimiter, group_zero_count, app_order_executed, app_orders_sequenced, app_message_resent_as_poss_dup, app_mixed_resend_gapfill_then_possdup, acceptor_initiated_logout, required_field_missing, incorrect_enum_value, invalid_tag_number, tag_not_defined_for_msg_type, tag_specified_without_value, incorrect_data_format, unregistered_msg_type) — note that's 17 listed; let me recount the 4.4 block: valid_new_order_accepted, group_count_mismatch, group_out_of_order, nested_group_missing_delimiter, group_zero_count, app_order_executed, app_orders_sequenced, app_message_resent_as_poss_dup, app_mixed_resend_gapfill_then_possdup, acceptor_initiated_logout, required_field_missing, incorrect_enum_value, invalid_tag_number, tag_not_defined_for_msg_type, tag_specified_without_value, incorrect_data_format, unregistered_msg_type = 17. Then 5 special (next_expected_msg_seq_num, last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency_timestamps, garbled_message_dropped, resend_request_chunk_size). **TOTAL = 46 + 7 + 17 + 5 = 75 scenarios.** (Each scenario runs against its `versions` vec — most version-parameterized ones carry a single version, so 75 scenario definitions → 75 (scenario,version) runs since the per-version ones are pre-split.)
- **Full scenario name list** (every name): 1a_ValidLogonWithCorrectMsgSeqNum, 1a_ValidLogonMsgSeqNumTooHigh, 2b_MsgSeqNumTooHigh, 2c_MsgSeqNumTooLow, 4b_ReceivedTestRequest, 13b_UnsolicitedLogoutMessage, 1d_LogonResponseResetFlag, 1c_LogonAdoptsHeartBtInt, 2f_ResendRequestGapFill, 2f3_ResendRequestBoundedEnd, 2_ResendRequestNotDuplicated, 2_ResendRequestBeginZeroIgnored, 2x_OutOfOrderQueuedThenDrained, 2g_SequenceResetReset, 2d_SequenceResetGapFillAdvances, 2e_SequenceResetGapFillBackwardIgnored, 2h_ResendRequestNothingToResend, 3a_ReceivedRejectConsumed, 0a_ReceivedHeartbeatConsumed, 2_MissingMsgSeqNum, 0_IdleHeartbeatEmitted, 4_TestRequestOnSilence, 2m_PossDupMsgSeqNumTooLow (×2 versions); 14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted_42, 14b_RequiredFieldMissing_42, 14e_IncorrectEnumValue_42, 14a_InvalidTagNumber_42, 14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType_42, 14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue_42, 14f_IncorrectDataFormat_42; 14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted, 14i_RepeatingGroupCountNotEqual, 14j_OutOfOrderRepeatingGroupMembers, QFJ934_MissingDelimiterNestedRepeatingGroup, 21_RepeatingGroupSpecifierWithValueOfZero, app_NewOrderSingleExecuted, app_OrdersOutboundSequenced, app_MessageResentAsPossDup, app_MixedResendGapFillThenPossDup, 5_AcceptorInitiatedLogout, 14b_RequiredFieldMissing, 14e_IncorrectEnumValue, 14a_InvalidTagNumber, 14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType, 14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue, 14f_IncorrectDataFormat, 2r_UnregisteredMsgType; special_NextExpectedMsgSeqNum, special_LastMsgSeqNumProcessed, special_CheckLatencyStaleSendingTime, special_GarbledMessageDropped, special_ResendRequestChunkSize.
- **Versions**: FIX.4.2 and FIX.4.4 only (FIX 4.0/4.1/4.3/4.4/5.0/SP1/SP2/FIXT NOT in AT suite).
- **Categories**: logon/sequencing, admin handling, resend/gap-fill, out-of-order queueing, field-validation reject family (4.2 + 4.4), special suites (789 NextExpectedMsgSeqNum, 369 LastMsgSeqNumProcessed, CheckLatency, garbled/checksum, resendRequestChunkSize), application-execution scenarios (executor app replying ExecutionReports). Runner is black-box over a real transport acceptor (fresh acceptor per scenario/version with per-scenario `SessionTweaks`).

### i) truefix facade (`crates/truefix/src/lib.rs`, examples/)
- Re-exports: core (decode/encode/DecodeError/Field/FieldError/FieldMap/Group/Message/MessageCracker/MessageFactory), session (Action/Application/Event/Role/Session/SessionConfig/SessionId/SessionState), transport (connect_initiator/Acceptor/SessionHandle), config. Adds `Engine` (start from SessionSettings → acceptors+initiators, all-or-nothing, builds stores) + `EngineError` (Config/Store/Io).
- **ApplicationFunctionalAdapter / ApplicationExtended**: NOT present.
- **Examples** (4): executor.rs, banzai.rs, multi_acceptor.rs, ordermatch.rs (plus tests: examples_smoke.rs, config_start.rs). No new examples beyond the 4 listed in README.

### j) New crates/modules
No crates beyond the 9 listed. thrdpty/ exists at workspace root (third-party, outside crates/ — not read per scope).

### Cross-cutting search confirmations (crates/)
- **failover/alternate/SocketConnectHost1/ConnectEndpointSet/connect_endpoints**: ABSENT (no failover).
- **proxy/PROXY protocol**: ABSENT (Unsupported).
- **mongo/MongoDB**: ABSENT.
- **fixlatest/FIX Latest**: ABSENT.
- **DoNotSend/Reject/BusinessReject as TYPE names**: PRESENT (typed structs in core error.rs) ✓. **RejectLogon**: ABSENT as a type. **FieldNotFound**: ABSENT (handled via dict RejectReason).
- **decode_with_dict**: ABSENT (equivalent is `decode_with_groups`).
- **from_settings/EngineConfig/builder**: `Engine` (not EngineConfig) in facade; `builder.rs` + `resolve()` in config; `build_store`/`build_log` exist.
- **schedule_reset.rs**: ABSENT (logic inline in transport).
- **FileStoreSync honored**: NO (`Rec`, unimplemented).
- **metrics as dependency**: declared in workspace deps but **NO crate depends on it**; no metrics exporter.
- **rustls_pemfile / rustls-pemfile**: in workspace deps, **NOT used in any crate** (no settings→TLS parsing).
- **TimeStampPrecision honored**: YES (enum + time_util formatting) though `now_utc_timestamp()` hardcodes Milliseconds.
- **PersistMessages honored**: YES (session.send_stored + transport persist_sent skip).
- **CheckCompID implemented**: config field exists; **enforcement NOT found** in state machine (comp-id not actually validated against session).
- **ReverseRoute/reverse_route**: ABSENT.
- **Component in dict model**: ABSENT (groups only).
- **session latency benchmark**: ABSENT (only codec.rs bench in truefix-core).
- **ApplicationFunctionalAdapter**: ABSENT.
- **MIGRATION**: MIGRATION.md exists at root (not in crates/); not searched in crates.

### Notable gaps vs. stated 002-qfj-parity-completion goals
SQL is SQLite-only (PG/MySQL features compiled but unused); TLS not parsed from settings (rustls-pemfile unused); no metrics exporter; no failover; MessageCracker/MessageFactory remain skeletons (no typed codegen structs); CheckCompID/RejectGarbledMessage/many log switches Recognized-but-unimplemented; CachedFileStore not differentiated; FileStoreSync unimplemented; AT suite covers only FIX.4.2/4.4.

Next steps:
If driving toward 002 parity: (1) implement PG/MySQL branches in truefix-store/sql.rs and truefix-log/sql.rs (sqlx features already enabled); (2) wire rustls-pemfile-based TLS config parsing in truefix-config/builder.rs so SocketKeyStore/TrustStore/NeedClientAuth produce rustls configs passed to transport; (3) add a `metrics`-dependent exporter crate or module; (4) enforce CheckCompID and implement RejectGarbledMessage in session state.rs; (5) decide on typed codegen structs for MessageCracker/MessageFactory or document them as intentionally skeleton; (6) honor FileStoreSync and the Recognized log switches; (7) expand AT suite to more FIX versions if full-version parity is required.

# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }

response.output:
# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J — first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: 🚧 Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** — stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** — the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** — protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields — they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI ─▶ S1 Core codec ─▶ S2 Minimal session ─▶ S3 Recovery/resend ─▶ S4 Data dictionary
   ─▶ S5 Store/log ─▶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect ─▶ S7 All versions + codegen
   ─▶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring ─▶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator ↔ acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) → protocol correctness → production-readiness → feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

use Stance::{Implemented as Impl, Recognized as Rec, Unsupported as Unsup};

/// The full Appendix A configuration-key baseline.
pub const APPENDIX_A_KEYS: &[KeyInfo] = &[
    // Session identity & type
    k("BeginString", "identity", Impl),
    k("SenderCompID", "identity", Impl),

/// Look up the stance for a configuration key (case-sensitive, as in QuickFIX/J).
pub fn key_info(name: &str) -> Option<&'static KeyInfo> {
    APPENDIX_A_KEYS.iter().find(|k| k.name == name)
}

# Migration Notes

Breaking-change and upgrade notes for TrueFix. Per Constitution Principle I, breaking changes to the
public API are recorded here with a semantic-version bump (FR-028).

## Unreleased — feature 002 (QuickFIX/J parity completion)

### Application callback signatures are now typed (breaking) — FR-016 / US5

The `Application` trait's fault-returning callbacks change from opaque `Result<(), String>` to typed
outcomes carrying a reason code and optional reference tag:

| Callback | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| `from_admin` | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), Reject>` (logon/admin refusal → 35=3) |
| `to_app`     | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), DoNotSend>` (suppress outbound) |
| `from_app`   | `Result<(), String>` | `Result<(), BusinessReject>` (→ 35=j) |

`Ok(())` is unchanged. An `Err("...")` becomes the matching typed variant:

- `Reject { reason, ref_tag, text }` — `reason` is the numeric SessionRejectReason (tag 373).
- `DoNotSend` — marker; the engine omits the message and does not store it as sent.
- `BusinessReject { reason, ref_tag, text }` — `reason` is the numeric BusinessRejectReason (tag 380).

These types are exported from `truefix_core` (re-exported by the `truefix` facade). Implementors update
their callback bodies to return the typed variants. No back-compat string variants are retained.

> Status: types landed (Setup/Foundational, stage G-foundational); trait signatures and engine effects
> are wired in stage G5 (US5). This section is finalised in T080 alongside the version bump.

args.result:
## TrueFix Codebase — Exhaustive Analysis (July 2026)

### Overview
Layered async-tokio FIX engine workspace (edition 2021, MSRV 1.96, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide; `unwrap/expect/panic/indexing_slicing` denied in non-test builds per crate). 9 crates, 90 `.rs` files. All crates at `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`. README still self-labels "pre-implementation (design complete)" but code is substantially implemented.

### a) truefix-core (`crates/truefix-core/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, error, message, field, field_map, group, tags, cracker, factory, framing, codec/{mod,decode,encode}; tests: field_types, garbled, groups, reference_vectors, roundtrip, versions; bench: codec.rs.
- **Types**: `Field` (raw bytes, typed accessors: as_str/int/decimal/char/bool/utc_timestamp; picosecond-accepting timestamp parser truncating to ns), `FieldMap` (ordered `Member::{Field, Group}` preserving wire order), `Group`+`GroupSpec` trait, `Message` (header/body/trailer FieldMaps).
- **Errors** (`error.rs`): `FieldError`, `DecodeError`, **`Reject`** (struct: reason/ref_tag/text — SessionRejectReason), **`DoNotSend`** (struct, suppresses send+seq), **`BusinessReject`** (struct: reason/ref_tag/text — BusinessRejectReason). All three exist as **typed** error structs (FR-016). `FieldNotFound`/`RejectLogon` type names do NOT exist (RejectLogon absent; field-not-found is handled via dict `RejectReason::RequiredTagMissing`, not a type).
- **MessageCracker** (`cracker.rs`): **skeleton only** — a trait with `fn crack(&mut self, message: &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>`; docstring says "Full typed dispatch over the generated messages arrives with the dictionary codegen (Stage S4)". NOT typed.
- **MessageFactory** (`factory.rs`): skeleton trait only.
- **Codec**: `decode` (flat fields), `decode_with_groups` (dict-driven group parsing via `GroupSpec` — works, greedy, recursive nested groups), `encode` (computes BodyLength/CheckSum, canonical order). `decode_with_dict` name does NOT exist (the equivalent is `decode_with_groups`). Group parsing from wire works.
- **Binary data fields**: `data_field_for_length` handles length→data field pairs (RawDataLength/RawData etc.); tokenizer honors embedded SOH.
- **framing.rs**: `frame_length` via BodyLength + 7-byte CheckSum trailer.
- **No reverse-route helper** in core.

### b) truefix-dict (`crates/truefix-dict/src/*.rs`, build.rs)
Files: lib, model, parser, validate, fixt, hash, build.rs; dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict (FIX40/41/42/43/44/FIXT11/FIX50/FIX50SP1/FIX50SP2). Tests: dual_track, fixt, group_decode, group_validation, parse, rejection_layers, toggles, versions.
- **Codegen** (`build.rs`): generates per-version `*_DICT_HASH: u64` (FNV-1a) + `pub mod {version}_msgs { pub const NEW_ORDER_SINGLE: &str = "D"; ... }` — **MsgType string constants only, NO typed structs**. Runtime asserts parsed hash == generated hash (dual-track proof, `dual_track` test).
- **Model** (`model.rs`): `DataDictionary` (fields/messages/header/trailer/groups/hash), `FieldDef`, `FieldType` (17 variants), `MessageDef` (required/optional/member_tags), `GroupDef` (count_tag/delimiter/members), `RejectReason` enum (10 variants with `.code()`), `ValidationError` (session vs business flag), **`ValidationOptions`** struct.
- **Validation toggles present in ValidationOptions**: `validate_fields_have_values`, `validate_user_defined_fields`, `allow_unknown_msg_fields`, `check_required_fields`, `check_field_types`, `check_groups`, **`first_field_in_group_is_delimiter`** ✓, **`validate_unordered_group_fields`** ✓. NOTE: `CheckCompID` and `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder` are NOT in ValidationOptions (out-of-order top-level fields not validated here).
- **validate.rs**: `impl GroupSpec for DataDictionary`; `validate()` does two rejection layers (session-level for tag/format/enum/required/group-structure; business-level for unknown MsgType). Recursive nested-group validation with delimiter + ordering checks.
- **FIXT** (`fixt.rs`): `FixtDictionaries` (transport + BTreeMap of application dicts by ApplVerID + DefaultApplVerID fallback + `application_for()`).
- **Custom dictionary loading**: `parse(&str)` from any normalized source; bundled `load_fixNN()` helpers; `ALL_DICTS` const. (Custom XML/.dict loading is NOT present — only the normalized `.fixdict` format.)
- **Component model**: NO `Component` type — groups only (no FIX component reusability abstraction).
- **FIX Latest / fixlatest**: NOT present (versions are FIX40–FIX50SP2 + FIXT11).

### c) truefix-session (`crates/truefix-session/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, config, state, admin, application, schedule, session_id, tags, time_util; tests: state_machine, recovery, restart_resend, validation_hook, timeouts, schedule.
- **SessionConfig** (`config.rs`) contains: begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, role, heartbeat_interval, reset_on_logon/logout/disconnect, refresh_on_logon, **persist_messages** ✓, resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency, max_latency, **check_comp_id** ✓ (field exists, default true), heartbeat_timeout_multiplier ✓, **timestamp_precision** (enum TimeStampPrecision: Seconds/Milli/Micro/Nano) ✓, logon_timeout, logout_timeout, reconnect_interval, schedule.
- **MessageStore integration**: NOT in Session — Session uses an in-memory `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` store + `BTreeMap<u64, Message>` out-of-order queue. The persistent `MessageStore` is wired in at the **transport** layer (`Services.store`), which calls `session.seed_sequences()` and `session.seed_sent_messages()` on connect, and persists via `persist_sent()`. So persistence is transport-driven, not Session-owned.
- **state.rs (sans-IO state machine)**: `Event::{Connected, Received, Tick, StartLogout}`, `Action::{Send, Disconnect}`, `SessionState::{Disconnected, AwaitingLogon, LoggedOn, AwaitingLogout}`. Implements: logon (initiator/acceptor, ResetSeqNumFlag, HeartBtInt adoption, NextExpectedMsgSeqNum 789 resend), seq-gap detection (too-high queues + single ResendRequest with resend-pending guard; too-low → Logout+Disconnect unless PossDup), ResendRequest handling (admin→GapFill, app→PossDup replay with OrigSendingTime), SequenceReset-GapFill/Reset, out-of-order queue drain, TestRequest→Heartbeat, idle Heartbeat timer, heartbeat-timeout disconnect (2*hb+2 ticks), CheckLatency (stale SendingTime→Logout), dictionary validation hook (`validate_app` → session Reject or BusinessMessageReject).
- **RejectGarbledMessage**: NOT implemented — transport `drain_messages` silently drops garbled frames ("Garbled frames are dropped at this stage; RejectGarbledMessage arrives in S3" — still a TODO). `CheckCompID`: config field exists but I found no comp-id validation enforcement in state.rs (latency checked; comp-id appears unenforced). `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`: not enforced at session level.
- **Switches honored**: PersistMessages ✓, HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier ✓ (hardcoded `2*hb+2` rather than using config field — config field set but logic uses literal `2`), TimeStampPrecision ✓ (time_util.rs formats per precision, though `now_utc_timestamp()` defaults to Milliseconds and `now_utc_timestamp_prec` exists), NOT honored: SendRedundantResendRequests (no field), ClosedResendInterval (no field), ResetOnError (no field), DisconnectOnError (no field), DisableHeartBeatCheck (no field), TestRequestDelayMultiplier (no field). These are all listed as `Rec` (Recognized) in config keys.rs.
- **schedule.rs**: `Schedule` (non_stop / daily start-end window wrapping midnight / weekdays filter / utc_offset_seconds). `is_in_session()`. No named IANA timezones (whole-second offset only); no StartDay/EndDay weekly windows.
- **schedule_reset.rs**: does NOT exist as a file. Scheduled reset handled inline in transport's `run_scheduled_initiator` (reset store on entering a window).
- **Reverse routing**: NOT implemented (no reverse_route helper; comp IDs are stamped from config).
- **admin.rs**: builders for Logon/Logout/Heartbeat/TestRequest/ResendRequest/SequenceReset/Reject/reject_with_reason/business_message_reject/prepare_resend.

### d) truefix-store (`crates/truefix-store/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, file, memory, noop, sql; test: restart_resend.
- **Backends**: `MemoryStore`, `FileStore`, `CachedFileStore`, `NoopStore`, `SqlStore` (behind `sql` feature). `MessageStore` trait (next_sender/target_seq, set_*, save, get, reset). `StoreConfig` enum + `build_store()`.
- **FileStoreSync**: NOT honored — `FileStore` uses synchronous std `fs` append (`OpenOptions::append`) on every `save`; `FileStoreSync` is `Rec` in keys.rs, not implemented; no fsync control.
- **CachedFileStore**: differentiated as a wrapper struct `CachedFileStore(FileStore)` but **delegates entirely** to FileStore (identical impl, no write-batching yet — docstring says optimizations "will live" there).
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore**: partially — `FileStore::was_corrupted()` flag + corrupt-trailing-record recovery on open (`parse_records` tolerates truncation). `ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore` key itself is `Rec`, not wired as a switch.
- **SQL**: `SqlStore` is **SQLite-only** (`SqliteConnectOptions`/`SqlitePool`), despite Cargo.toml enabling `postgres`+`mysql` sqlx features. NO PG/MySQL code paths. Hardcoded table names `seqnums`/`messages` (no custom table names — `JdbcStoreMessagesTableName` etc. are `Rec`, unused). `SqlitePool::connect_with` only — **no explicit connection-pool sizing config** (JdbcMaxActiveConnection etc. are `Rec`, unused).

### e) truefix-log (`crates/truefix-log/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, file, screen, composite, tracing_log, sql; test: composite, sql_log.
- `Log` trait (on_incoming/on_outgoing/on_event, sync+infallible). `LogConfig` enum + `build_log()`. `ScreenLog`/`FileLog`(messages.log+event.log)/`TracingLog`/`CompositeLog`/`SqlLog`(sql feature).
- **Log config switches NOT implemented**: `FileLogHeartbeats`, `ScreenLogShowEvents`, `ScreenLogShowHeartBeats`, `ScreenLogShowIncoming`, `ScreenLogShowOutgoing`, `FileIncludeMilliseconds`, etc. are all `Rec` in keys.rs and NOT honored — ScreenLog/FileLog unconditionally log all streams. No filtering.
- **SQL log**: SQLite-only (`SqlLog` writes to `log_messages`/`log_events` via a background tokio task over an unbounded mpsc channel). NO PG/MySQL. No custom table names (`JdbcLogIncomingTable` etc. `Rec`, unused).

### f) truefix-transport (`crates/truefix-transport/src/*.rs`)
Files: lib, framing; tests: auth, integration_logon, monitor, multi_dynamic, reconnect, restart_continuity, scheduled, tls.
